content.problemStatement:
A string SSS is called good if and only if :


The number of occurrences of  "ab\text{ab}ab" as a substring of SSS === the number of occurrences of "ba\text{ba}ba" as a substring of SSS.
For example, aaba\text{aaba}aaba, a\text{a}a and babab\text{babab}babab are good strings, while ab\text{ab}ab is not.

You are given a string SSS consisting of characters a\text{a}a and b\text{b}b only. Find the length of the largest subsequence†^{\dagger}† of the string SSS which is good.

†^{\dagger}† TTT is said to be a subsequence of SSS, if we can obtain TTT by deleting some elements from SSS, and concatenating the remaining portion without changing their order. For example, ab\text{ab}ab, aa\text{aa}aa, a\text{a}a and aba\text{aba}aba are all subsequences of aba\text{aba}aba, but bb\text{bb}bb and baa\text{baa}baa are not.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains NNN - the size of the string
The second line contains SSS - the string




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the length of the largest subsequence of TTT which is good.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : abba\text{abba}abba has 111 occurrence of ab\text{ab}ab and 111 occurrence of ba\text{ba}ba. Thus, it is already a good string. Hence, the answer is simply 444.
Test Case 2 : ab\text{ab}ab is not good as it has 111 occurrence of ab\text{ab}ab but none of ba\text{ba}ba. a\text{a}a is a good string, which has size 111.
